title: allium albanicum
intro: Allium albanicum is a species of wild garlic, also known as Albanian leek, native to the Balkan Peninsula. It belongs to the Allium genus, which includes various species of garlic and onions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. Allium albanicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering potential health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function.
